formatLabel: Premier-style construction (exactly 50) · owned SOR + ASH card pool · Eternal/casual legal
notes: Every list uses only exact owned cards and printings, with zero aspect penalties. ASH is current, but SOR has rotated from Premier; because many lists mix both sets, this portfolio is presented for Eternal and casual play. Collector scores use the dated public card-database snapshot and are never fetched at runtime.

decks.name: Phoenix Ignition
decks.identity: This deck wins by flooding the board with cheap Rebels and letting Sabine's unavoidable base damage finish what coordinated attacks start, before big defenders stabilize.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest lightly (8 space units) — Green Squadron A-Wing and Alliance X-Wing apply pressure while the ground board races.

guide.winCondition: Deal 30 base damage by round 5-6: wide Rebel boards, Fleet Lieutenant attack triggers, and Sabine's leader tick every round.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: play a unit every action. SpecForce Soldier strips Sentinel from a blocker; Battlefield Marine is your best rate. Use Sabine's leader action every round — 10+ guaranteed damage over a game. Attack the base unless a Sentinel or Restore unit forces a trade.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-4: deploy Sabine (4 resources) once the board can protect her; her On Attack adds another base tick. Fleet Lieutenant and Rebel Assault convert your width into extra attacks. Wing Leader's 2 Experience makes a Marine or Echo Base Defender trade up.
guide.lateGame: Round 5+: Zeb clears a blocker and his 4-damage bonus removes the next one. Mace Windu re-readies on kills to break stalled boards. If the race is close, count Sabine ticks plus Saboteur attacks — Precision Fire ignores Sentinel for exactly this.
mulligan: Keep: two units costing 1-2 plus any 3-cost follow-up.
Keep: one early unit + Fleet Lieutenant + a 2-drop.
Send back: hands with two or more cards costing 4+ — this deck cannot afford dead early turns.
Send back: hands relying on the single Rebel Assault to perform.

combos.note: Fleet Lieutenant's When Played attack gives a Rebel +2/+0 — a Marine hits for 5 the turn it's already in play.
2 Experience turns the 4/3 Sentinel into a 6/5 wall the round it lands.
Both ignore Sentinel; Precision Fire also adds +2/+0 to Troopers, so a Pathfinder hits the base for 4.
Zeb removes the blocker and deals 4 more to the next one; Sabine's tick makes every stabilization attempt one round too slow.

resourcePriorities: Resource Mace Windu or a second Wampa early
Keep every 1-2 cost unit
Regional Sympathizers is the first mid-cost card to resource in fast matchups
weaknesses: Less burn/reach than historical Sabine lists — Red Three, K-2SO and For a Cause I Believe In are missing, so the deck leans harder on board retention.
Board sweepers and wide removal (Overwhelming Barrage, AT-AT Suppressor) break the go-wide plan.
Only one Rebel Assault: treat it as a bonus, not an engine.
adjustments: Medal Ceremony (owned x3) over Keep Fighting for a wider Experience payoff after alpha strikes.
Karabast or Heroic Sacrifice (owned x1 each) as extra reach if local metas run big Sentinels.
Guerilla Attack Pod (owned x2) as a top-end swap for Mace Windu in grindier fields.
acquisitions: Red Three (SOR) — Raid enabler that historical Sabine lists leaned on.
K-2SO (SOR) — resilient pressure plus direct base damage.
For a Cause I Believe In (SOR) — burst reach that closes stalled races.

decks.name: Painted Hyperdrive
decks.identity: This deck wins by splitting pressure across both arenas and using Cunning tricks to remove exactly one blocking action per round until Sabine's clock ends the game.
decks.spacePolicy: Dominate — 23 space units make space the primary lane; ground is a distraction the opponent cannot ignore because of Sabine's ticks.

guide.winCondition: Space damage plus Sabine's guaranteed base ticks. Shoot First and Surprise Strike win the two or three fights that matter; everything else hits the base.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: Mining Guild TIE and Outer Rim Headhunter contest space immediately; SpecForce Soldier and Leia Organa — Defiant Princess hold ground. Use Sabine's action every round.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-4: deploy Sabine early (4 resources). With a leader unit in play, Outer Rim Headhunter exhausts a defender on each attack — pick the blocker that would trade profitably. Auzituck Liberator Gunship's Ambush removes their best space unit.
guide.lateGame: Round 5+: Black One refuels an empty hand (discard, draw 3). Waylay their stabilizer the round it lands; a bounced 6-drop is usually game over on tempo. Count exact lethal: Surprise Strike is +3 reach.
mulligan: Keep: a 1-cost space unit plus any second cheap body and one trick.
Keep: Leia Organa — Defiant Princess plus a 2-cost space unit (her ready-a-resource effectively plays both).
Send back: hands with two or more 4+ cost cards or three tricks and one unit.

combos.note: Once Sabine deploys, every Headhunter attack also exhausts a non-leader unit — a free No Good to Me Dead each round.
First-strike damage plus her On Attack ping removes 2-3 HP blockers without taking damage back.
Attach for one resource and attack immediately — an Ambush unit effectively attacks twice in one round.
Exhausts their best attacker and shields the Ace, which has grown from every card you played that phase.

resourcePriorities: Resource Black One early if your hand is full of cheap action
Keep Waylay for their 5-6 cost play
Excess Snapshot Reflexes is resource fodder
weaknesses: Temporary answers (exhaust, bounce) turn into card disadvantage if the game goes past round 6-7.
A single wide sweep or two large Sentinels in space can invalidate half the deck's damage.
Light interaction against upgrades and Restore engines.
adjustments: Disarm and Sneak Attack (owned x1 each) add trick density against unit-heavy fields.
Wing Leader can become Star Wing Scout x1 + Vanguard Ace tuning in faster metas.
acquisitions: Millennium Falcon — Piece of Junk (SOR) — the premier tempo space threat this shell is missing.
Red Three (SOR) — buffs every Raid attack in the deck.

decks.name: Bounty Compound Interest
decks.identity: This deck wins midrange fights by converting every enemy unit that leaves play into an extra resource through Boba's leader ability, then banking that tempo into premium threats the opponent must answer.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest with 12 space units, half of which (Gladiator, Relentless) double as finishers; Waylay and exhaust effects answer what slips through.

guide.winCondition: Efficient trades plus Boba's refunded resources put you a full play ahead by round 4; Darth Vader, Gladiator Star Destroyer and Relentless close before the opponent recovers.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: ISB Agent (reveal an event for a ping) and Viper Probe Droid trade early. Shoot First or a well-timed trade triggers Boba: an enemy unit left play, so ready a resource and squeeze in a 1-cost play the same round.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-4: No Good to Me Dead locks the defender Boba Fett — Disintegrator wants to attack: an exhausted unit that stayed in play takes his +3 On Attack, killing almost any 3-4 drop. Superlaser Technician trades and ramps. Kallus ambushes into their board and draws on unique defeats.
guide.lateGame: Round 5+: Darth Vader — Commanding the First Legion pulls ISB Agents, Snowtrooper Lieutenants or a Superlaser Technician out of the deck for free while ambushing. Relentless blanks their first event each round — deploy it when their hand is stacked with removal. Overwhelming Barrage is the board-swing finisher.
mulligan: Keep: an early unit, a 3-cost play and one removal/trick.
Keep: Superlaser Technician hands — he is both curve and ramp.
Send back: hands with two or more 6+ cost cards; the deck earns its top end, it does not open with it.

combos.note: The lock keeps the unit exhausted through the regroup phase, so Disintegrator's +3 trigger is guaranteed next round.
Bounce counts as leaving play: undo their tempo play and get a resource refund on top.

cards: Darth Vader — Commanding the First Legion
combos.note: Vader's search can drop Technicians that later convert into resources — ramp attached to a 5/7 Ambush body.
The Crew's When Played recovers your best spent event; Barrage twice ends most boards.
Gladiator grants Sentinel to protect Kallus, who keeps drawing as unique units die around him.

resourcePriorities: Resource Relentless or I Had No Choice before round 4
Keep exactly one big threat in hand at all times
Never resource removal against midrange
weaknesses: No Energy Conversion Lab or Devastator, so the historical Boba Command top-end is approximated, not replicated.
One copy each of the marquee legendaries — drawing them is a luxury, not the plan.
Fast go-wide aggro can outpace the value engine if early trades are declined.
adjustments: Bib Fortuna (owned x1) discounts your event suite in slower metas.
Blizzard Assault AT-AT (owned, incl. Hyperspace/foil printings) as an alternative finisher over Relentless.
Cartel Spacer (owned x1) adds an exhaust effect that also feeds the Disintegrator lock.
acquisitions: Fett's Firespray (SOR) — the defining Boba tempo vehicle this list lacks.
Cunning (SOR) — the flexible double-Cunning event, playable here penalty-free only with a Cunning base swap.
Traitorous (SOR) — steal effects convert Boba triggers twice.

decks.name: Inferno Redline
decks.identity: Iden heals the base while a dense Vigilance package trades repeatedly; the two newly owned copies of Vigilance are now the deck's reset button.
decks.spacePolicy: Imperial ships establish early space presence while ground Sentinels buy time for Vigilance.

guide.winCondition: Trade, heal with Iden, reset a developed arena with Vigilance, then finish behind large Imperial units.
guide.earlyGame: Contest the opponent's first arena and plan a defeat that triggers Iden's heal.
guide.midGame: Use removal sparingly so Vigilance can produce a decisive multi-mode swing.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Iden after stabilizing and let repeated healing put the race out of reach.
openingPriorities: Keep a cost-2 unit and one removal event.
Against aggro, Guardian of the Whills is a priority.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: The reset plus leader healing reverses both board and base pressure.
The two answers cover both a developed arena and an individual large threat.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Cunning tempo can delay expensive stabilizers.
Passive hands without a cost-2 unit fall behind.
adjustments: Against control, resource early removal.
Against aggro, keep every cheap unit and heal trigger.

decks.name: Project Stardust Grind
decks.identity: This deck wins long board fights by making damaged Imperial units better than clean ones — Krennic's +1/+0, Experience from the Walker, and Superlaser ramp grind out value every exchange.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest with 8 space bodies (System Patrol Craft Sentinels, Gladiators); Vanquish and Takedown cover what they cannot.

guide.winCondition: Favorable trades all game: damaged survivors get +1/+0 under Krennic, Superlaser Technicians convert deaths into ramp, and Blizzard Assault AT-AT or Relentless arrive a round early to end it.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: Scout Bike Pursuer (Grit) and Viper Probe Droid trade; Death Trooper's self-damage is a feature — the damaged unit gets +1/+0 from Krennic and Grit stacks. Resupply on round 2-3 if the hand is heavy.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-5: Grand Moff Tarkin — Death Star Overseer refills with 2 Imperial units. Deploy Krennic at 5: his Restore 2 plus the anthem flips every trade. Academy Defense Walker hands out Experience to your battle-scarred board.
guide.lateGame: Round 6+: Blizzard Assault AT-AT's excess damage cleaves through chump blockers; Relentless shuts off their answer events; The Emperor's Legion returns a traded wave for round-after-round pressure. Darth Vader — Commanding the First Legion swings any board he ambushes into.
openingPriorities: Grand Moff Tarkin — Death Star Overseer
mulligan: Keep: a 2-cost body, a 3-cost play and ramp (Resupply/Technician).
Keep: Death Trooper plus Scout Bike Pursuer — the self-damage line is your strongest start.
Send back: hands of only 6+ cost ships and walkers, even with Resupply.

combos.note: Self-damaging your own trooper turns Krennic's anthem on: a 4/1 that already killed a 2-drop.
Experience on damaged units: the Grit bike becomes a 3/5+ Sentinel-protected attacker.
Technician deaths ramp you to an 8-cost 9/9 a full round early.
Trade your whole board, Barrage theirs, then return your units to hand for two more rounds of bodies.

resourcePriorities: Resource Relentless/Vader if drawn before round 4
Keep Vanquish against ramp decks
Inferno Four is flex — resource it when the curve is set
weaknesses: Krennic rewards damaged survivors: mass hard removal (Vanquish effects) that skips the damage step wastes his bonus.
Ramp draws without payoffs (or payoffs without ramp) are the main losing pattern.
Little reach — the deck wins on board, not burn, so Restore-heavy decks prolong games.
adjustments: Rukh (owned x2) — second copy over Inferno Four against unique midrange.
97th Legion (owned x2) scales with the ramp plan if games go past round 7 in your meta.
Lieutenant Childsen (owned x1) adds a Sentinel that grows with your Vigilance hand.
acquisitions: Devastator (SOR) — the top-end this archetype was built around historically.
Superlaser Blast (SOR) — converts the defensive shell into true control.

decks.name: The Senate's Long Game
decks.identity: Palpatine now uses Command rather than Cunning, incorporating the newly owned Command legendary into a cleaner Imperial ramp shell.
decks.spacePolicy: Cheap TIEs protect space while Superlaser Technician and Command accelerate the ground-heavy endgame.

guide.winCondition: Ramp ahead, spend expendable Imperial units for value, then bury the opponent under Palpatine and capital ships.
guide.earlyGame: Play a cheap Imperial unit or Superlaser Technician; avoid spending removal on replaceable bodies.
guide.midGame: Use Command to recover a unit while accelerating the resource plan.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Palpatine or a capital ship and convert the resource lead into repeated two-for-one turns.
openingPriorities: Keep Superlaser Technician, Resupply, or a complete unit curve.
Command is a keep only with early development.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: The Technician is an ideal expendable unit and advances the expensive endgame.
The event bridges resources and recovers a high-impact Imperial threat.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Fast Heroism aggro can punish ramp turns.
Expensive hands can fail to affect the first two turns.
adjustments: Against aggro, resource capital ships and trade early.
Against control, preserve Command for recursion value.

decks.name: March of the First Legion
decks.identity: This deck wins by grinding out early boards with cheap Imperials and leader pings, then deploying Darth Vader into a board he immediately dominates.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest with 8 space units (Interceptors, TIE Advanced, Gladiators); Imperial Interceptor's 3-damage ping polices their space on arrival.

guide.winCondition: Vader's leader action (1 damage to a unit + 1 to base every round you played a Villainy card) accumulates control and clock simultaneously; the deployed Vader plus walker top-end finishes.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: Death Star Stormtrooper trades up; Snowtrooper Lieutenant's When Played attack trigger with +2/+0 on an Imperial removes 2-3 HP units. Play a Villainy card every round so Vader's action is never wasted.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-5: Veers makes the swarm real; Force Choke and Open Fire clear the mid board. Vader's Lightsaber is held for his round-7 deployment when possible — attached to Darth Vader it deals 4 free damage. Ozzel cheats a Gladiator or AT-ST into play ready (accept the opponent's ready as the price of tempo).
guide.lateGame: Round 6+: deploy Vader — his On Attack 2-damage ping plus the Lightsaber usually kills two units in one action. Blizzard Assault AT-AT cleaves; Commanding the First Legion refills the board with free Villainy units mid-combat.
mulligan: Keep: two cheap Imperials plus removal or Veers.
Keep: any hand with turn-1 and turn-2 plays and one 4+ card maximum.
Send back: double 6+ hands; Vader is your top end, the deck does not need more.

cards: Darth Vader — Dark Lord of the Sith
combos.note: Played on the deployed Vader leader: 4 damage to a ground unit, then his On Attack pings 2 more — a 2-cost removal spell stapled to +3/+1.
Ozzel puts the Gladiator in ready; it grants Sentinel immediately and threatens an attack the same round.
The Lieutenant's attack trigger on a First Legion Snowtrooper into a damaged unit is +4/+0 with Overwhelm.

cards: Darth Vader — Commanding the First Legion
combos.note: Motti's death readies the 5/7 Vader unit for a second attack.

resourcePriorities: Resource Blizzard Assault AT-AT before round 4
Keep one removal against midrange
Maximum Firepower is the flex slot — resource it when behind
weaknesses: The leader deploys at 7: falling badly behind before then cannot always be repaired.
Anthem effects die to cheap removal; the swarm shrinks fast without Veers.
No Devastator/Energy Conversion Lab means the historical ramp finish is flattened into walkers.
adjustments: General Tagge (owned x1) is already the flex — swap for a 2nd Maximum Firepower in unit metas.
Occupier Siege Tank can become AT-ST (owned x4→using 2) if Overwhelm matters more than Grit.
acquisitions: Emperor's Royal Guard (SOR) — Sentinel body that protects the round Vader deploys.

decks.name: Kyber Will Not Break
decks.identity: This deck wins by making one durable Force body unkillable-in-practice — Chirrut himself, or a Kanan/Obi-Wan line — and converting upgrades into repeated favorable attacks.
decks.spacePolicy: Race it: only 3 space bodies. Restored ARC-170 heals the base while the ground plan executes; Precision Fire and Make an Opening answer space only when it truly threatens lethal.

guide.winCondition: Upgraded Force units win every combat: Jedi Lightsaber's -2/-2, Chirrut's +0/+2 leader pump, and shields/heals stack until attackers stop being profitable, then the 4-6 cost bodies take over the race.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: Guardian of the Whills discounts your first upgrade each round; SpecForce Soldier and Wilderness Fighter (Shielded) trade cleanly. Resilient on a Guardian makes an early wall.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-5: deploy Chirrut at 5 — during the action phase he simply refuses to die (0 HP doesn't defeat him until regroup), so aggressive blocks that would kill anything else leave him standing; heal or pump him before regroup and the opponent got nothing. Kanan attacks mill and heal per Spectre (Zeb counts).
guide.lateGame: Round 6+: Obi-Wan dies into 2 Experience + a card for a Force unit; The Force Is With Me turns any body into a shielded, experienced attacker mid-combat. Mace cleans up exhausted boards.
mulligan: Keep: a 1-2 cost body plus one upgrade and interaction.
Never keep: multiple upgrades without a unit to carry them.
Send back: hands where nothing costs less than 3.

cards: Chirrut Îmwe — One With the Force
combos.note: Chirrut survives the action phase at 0 HP; healing 3 before regroup makes their whole attack sequence a blank.
On a Force unit the saber gives the defender -2/-2 on attack: Yoda kills 3-HP units and takes nothing back.
Discounted Restore 2 turns a 2-drop into a heal engine the aggro matchup hates.
Two Spectres = 2 mills per Kanan attack, healing per aspect milled — real sustain, occasional information.
Obi-Wan's dying gift makes Mace a 7/9 that re-readies on kills.

resourcePriorities: Resource Mace/second Obi-Wan early
Keep Make an Opening for real threats
Force Throw is situational — resource against empty-hand aggro
weaknesses: Bounce and hard removal (Waylay, Vanquish, Power of the Dark Side) turn a 3-card investment into a 3-card loss.
Only one Force Throw and one Jedi Lightsaber owned — the deck cannot rely on drawing them.
No Fallen Lightsaber; the upgrade suite is a size down from historical Chirrut lists.
adjustments: Luke's Lightsaber (owned x3) if you also run Luke leader shells — here it stays out because Luke never enters play.
Entrenched (owned x1) on THEIR attacker is tech against Voltron mirrors.
Protector (owned x1, double-Vigilance) becomes penalty-free with a Vigilance base swap (Capital City/Dagobah Swamp).
acquisitions: Fallen Lightsaber (SOR) — the missing marquee upgrade for Force decks.
Luke Skywalker — Jedi Knight (SOR) — the top-end this shell dreams about.

decks.name: Two Fronts, One Rebellion
decks.identity: This deck wins by keeping two ready Rebels at all times and compressing two attacks into one Leia action, creating a damage-and-initiative advantage no defensive line can schedule around.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest lightly (9 space units); X-Wings and Wing Leader threaten enough that ground defenses cannot consolidate.

guide.winCondition: Leia's leader action attacks with two Rebels every round — effectively a free action. Anthem effects (Dodonna) and Experience (Wing Leader, Medal Ceremony) make those two attacks lethal math by round 5.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: deploy Rebels every action — R2-D2 and C-3PO are real cards here (droid bodies that fix draws). Battlefield Marine on curve. Use Leia's action as soon as two Rebels can attack profitably.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-5: deploy Leia at 5; her Raid 1 plus On Attack chains a second Rebel attack. Dodonna turns the whole board +1/+1. Medal Ceremony after a wide attack banks 3 Experience for zero cost.
guide.lateGame: Round 5+: Home One restores and revives a fallen Heroism unit at discount; Wedge gives every vehicle Ambush (+1/+1) for surprise lethal from hand. Guerilla Attack Pod readies itself when their base is at 15+ damage.
mulligan: Keep: two 1-2 cost Rebels and any 3-drop.
Keep: one-drop + Fleet Lieutenant + Dodonna curve.
Send back: hands with Home One/Wedge and fewer than two early Rebels.

combos.note: Fleet Lieutenant's When Played attack plus Leia's double-attack action: three attacks in two actions.
Her action attacks two Rebels; a 0-cost Medal Ceremony then gives both (plus one more) permanent Experience.

cards: Wedge Antilles — Star of the Rebellion
combos.note: Every vehicle in hand becomes an Ambush threat — an X-Wing arrives as a 3/4 that attacks immediately.
R2 peeks and can bottom the top card; 3PO then names the cost he saw for a free draw.
Revive Zeb for 2: a 5/5 that murders a blocker, under a ship that gives everything Restore.

resourcePriorities: Resource Home One/Guerilla Attack Pod before round 3
Keep Rebel Assault only with a board
Regional Sympathizers is the resource in fast mirrors
weaknesses: Sweepers and multi-unit removal break the two-ready-Rebels requirement Leia depends on.
Missing Red Three, K-2SO and For a Cause I Believe In: less reach than the historical list, so boards must stick.
Very few events — almost no answers; the deck must always be the aggressor.
adjustments: Medal Ceremony x3 (owned) in slower metas; the third copy replaces Guerilla Attack Pod.
Snowspeeder x3 (owned) over C-3PO pair if Vehicle synergy with Wedge matters more than consistency.
acquisitions: Red Three (SOR), K-2SO (SOR), For a Cause I Believe In (SOR) — the historical Leia aggro power trio.

decks.name: Kessel Run on Credit
decks.identity: This deck wins by borrowing tempo — Han smuggles a resource ahead every round it matters — and spending the loan on threats one round before the opponent's answers line up.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest with 15 space units; cheap Cunning fighters harass early while Home One and friends land ahead of schedule.

guide.winCondition: Resource advantage converted into oversized mid-game turns: a round-5 Home One or a round-4 Chewbacca wall stabilizes or snowballs, and Waylay/Shoot First protect the lead.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: Mining Guild TIE, Outer Rim Headhunter and Crafty Smuggler contest cheaply. Han's action puts a spare card into play as a ready resource — plan the discard-from-play cost: the loan comes due at the start of next action phase.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-5: Leia — Defiant Princess readies a resource (stacking with Han's loan for +2 tempo rounds). Resupply makes some loans permanent. Bright Hope bounces a spent unit for value and a draw; Escort Skiff ambushes with any Command unit around.
guide.lateGame: Round 5+: Home One a round early is usually decisive — it revives a Heroism unit on arrival. Han Solo — Reluctant Hero first-strikes through blockers. U-Wing Reinforcement flips a stalled board with up to 3 free units.
mulligan: Keep: an early unit plus ramp plus one payoff.
Keep: double 2-drop hands with Resupply.
Send back: payoff-only hands — Han's loan needs a board to protect the round it repays.
Send back: ramp with no payoff above cost 4 unless the rest curves perfectly.

combos.note: The loan plus Resupply reaches 8 resources on round 5; Home One arrives before their answers do.
Any other Command unit turns on the Skiff's Ambush — a 4/4 that attacks the moment it lands.
Re-buy the Insurgent's disruption When Played; Bright Hope draws you a card for doing it.
Sentinel that readies when attacked plus bounce on their follow-up: a two-card wall that costs them a whole round.

resourcePriorities: Han's action prefers duplicate mid-cost cards
Never loan-resource a card you will need back — it is defeated, not returned
U-Wing Reinforcement is a keep in grindy matchups only
weaknesses: The loan repayment (defeat a resource) makes sloppy planning lethal: a missed land-back turn can skip a whole play.
No Millennium Falcon — the historical Han payoff — so the top end leans on Home One alone.
Aggro decks can ignore the value game and race the loan turns.
adjustments: Syndicate Lackeys (owned x2) can become Cantina Bouncer + Strafing Gunship (owned x1 each) for more interaction.
Recruit (owned x5) count is flexible; a third copy smooths payoff finding.
acquisitions: Millennium Falcon — Piece of Junk (SOR) — the card this leader was designed to cheat out.
Vanguard Ace at full playset (own 2) — grows absurdly on loan turns.

decks.name: Hope in Hyperspace
decks.identity: This deck wins on shielded, resilient value units that trade up and heal back — while showing off 14 owned Hyperspace and foil printings without spending a single deck slot on a weak card.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest with 7 space units (System Patrol Craft Sentinels + ARC-170s); Make an Opening and Kallus cover the rest.

guide.winCondition: Every unit demands more resources to remove than it cost: Luke shields what you play, walls hold the ground, and Home One or an experienced Obi-Wan line closes long games.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: R2-D2 fixes draws; Battlefield Marine and Restored ARC-170 (Restore 1) start the HP race. Luke's leader action (pay 1) shields any Heroism unit you played that phase — use it nearly every round.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-5: Cloud City Wing Guard and Consular Security Force are the sturdiest walls in the pool; Echo Base Defender Sentinel stops aggro cold. Agent Kallus ambushes and starts drawing. Frontier AT-RT ambushes whenever another vehicle is around.
guide.lateGame: Round 6+: deploy Luke, attach Luke's Lightsaber — full heal plus shield makes him nearly unkillable, and his On Attack shields another unit every swing. Obi-Wan trades into 2 Experience + a card; Home One turns the accumulated value into a finish.
mulligan: Keep: two plays for rounds 1-2 plus one 3-4 cost wall.
Keep: any hand with early units and Make an Opening.
Send back: hands with Home One plus two other 4+ cards.

combos.note: On the deployed Luke: heal ALL damage, add a shield, and every later attack shields another unit — the namesake line of the deck.
Two Ambush vehicles that enable each other; the Snowspeeder also exhausts an enemy ground vehicle on attack.
Uniques die constantly in long games; Kallus banks a card each round while the Guards keep Sentinel up.
-2/-2 plus base heal, then Yoda's Restore attacks — this deck wins damage races it never seems to run.

resourcePriorities: Resource Home One before round 4
Recruit early to find curve, resource it late
Keep Repair against aggro, resource against control
weaknesses: Missing Luke Skywalker — Jedi Knight, the natural finisher — the late game is honest value, not a haymaker.
Low aggression: bad draws turn into durable-but-passive boards that lose to ramped finishers.
Shield-hate (Saboteur) blunts the leader's whole ability.
adjustments: All 14 non-Standard printings here are the strongest cosmetic build the collection supports without hurting the list; further foils exist only on off-aspect cards.
Fleet Lieutenant (owned x5) can replace 2-1B pair for a more aggressive slant.
A Capital City (Hyperspace, owned x2) base swap trades the Command splash for mono-Vigilance and makes the owned foil Vigilance event penalty-free.
acquisitions: Luke Skywalker — Jedi Knight (SOR) — the missing crown jewel, in Hyperspace if the collection is to stay showcase-first.

decks.name: Promotion by Fire
decks.identity: This deck wins by promoting cheap Imperials into veterans — Tarkin's Experience every round, Tagge and TIE Advanced stacking more — and cashing the swarm in before attrition catches up.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest with 9 space units; TIE Advanced doubles as ground-swarm support by dumping Experience on a trooper.

guide.winCondition: A wide Imperial board where every body is one Experience bigger than it should be, plus Tarkintown's epic ping and Maximum Firepower converting power into removal.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: lead with Death Star Stormtrooper/Snowtrooper Lieutenant; Tarkin's action promotes whichever unit the opponent must now answer. Viper Probe Droid scouts their plan.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-5: Veers turns the swarm sideways; Grand Moff Tarkin — Death Star Overseer refills with two more Imperials. First Legion Snowtrooper plus Tarkintown's epic action (3 damage to a damaged unit) executes anything mid-sized.
guide.lateGame: Round 5+: deploy Tarkin — his On Attack gives a second Experience every swing. Maximum Firepower with two veterans on board is 6-10 damage to one target. Occupier Siege Tank with Grit finishes damaged races.
mulligan: Keep: two 1-2 cost Imperials plus a 3-cost play.
Keep: aggressive hands even without payoffs — Tarkin's action IS the payoff.
Send back: hands with Superlaser Technician as the only early play (he is ramp, not defense).

combos.note: One Experience makes the 3/1 a 4/2 — suddenly it trades with 2-drops AND hits like a 4-drop.
TIE Advanced pumps a friendly Imperial by 2 Experience, then Firepower uses both units' power on one target.
The base pings a damaged unit for 3; the Snowtrooper then attacks it at +2/+0 with Overwhelm.

cards: Grand Moff Tarkin — Death Star Overseer
combos.note: Ozzel flashes in the tutor Tarkin ready — refill and a surprise attacker in one action.

resourcePriorities: Resource Occupier Siege Tank early
Superlaser Technician is a resource in every hand without ramp targets
Keep Force Choke for Sentinels
weaknesses: Removal erases invested Experience — do not over-promote one body.
Tarkintown's 25 HP means every race is 5 damage tighter; count both clocks every round.
Sweepers (none exist in SOR at rate, but Barrage-style effects) still beat width.
adjustments: Seasoned Shoretrooper scales if games go long — a 3rd copy is not owned; General Tagge (owned x1) fits the same slot.
Catacombs of Cadera (owned, 30 HP) is the safe-base variant when the meta is aggressive.
acquisitions: Emperor's Royal Guard (SOR) and Superlaser Blast (SOR) for a tougher long game.

decks.name: Walking Carpet Counterpunch
decks.identity: This deck wins by refusing to let the opponent pick fights — Chewbacca's leader action flashes in Sentinels mid-phase — then counterpunching with tricks once their attacks are spent.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest with 9 space units including Sentinel walls (System-free: Distant Patroller, ARC-170, Auzituck); Waylay answers capital-class threats.

guide.winCondition: Stabilize behind Sentinels and healing, generate small value edges (Obi-Wan, Yoda, 2-1B), then win the long game with Han's first-strike and an unanswered board.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: cheap bodies only if needed — the deck's trick is patience. Chewbacca's action plays a 3-or-less unit WITH Sentinel during the action phase: hold Cloud City Wing Guard and drop it exactly when their attacker commits.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-5: Vigilant Honor Guards + 2-1B (heal 2 on attack) keeps the Sentinel condition alive. Chewbacca — Loyal Companion readies whenever attacked — a wall that counterattacks. Leia DP and Waylay steal back the tempo their removal spent.
guide.lateGame: Round 6+: deploy Chewbacca (Grit Sentinel that grows as it's hit). Han Solo — Reluctant Hero ambushes and first-strikes through their best blocker; Obi-Wan's death converts into a bigger threat plus a card.
mulligan: Keep: early presence in at least one arena plus a 3-cost body for the leader action.
Keep: reactive hands with Waylay/Asteroid Sanctuary only alongside two units.
Send back: hands that cannot act before round 3.

combos.note: Flash Sentinel: their attacker is already committed when the wall appears, eating the hit on your terms.
Heal 2 keeps the Guards undamaged, which keeps their Sentinel online permanently.
A shielded ready-when-attacked Sentinel is a wall that wins every exchange it doesn't dodge.
First-strike ambush, then exhaust their counterattacker and shield Han's escort.

resourcePriorities: Resource Han/Obi-Wan duplicates early
Keep exactly two tricks; resource the rest
Distant Patroller is a resource after round 4
weaknesses: FFG's own retrospective rates Chewbacca among the weakest SOR leaders — this is a fun counterpunch deck, not a tournament pick (hence the 'Experimental' power band).
A 7-cost leader deploy arrives after many games are decided.
Reactive draws against control do very little; the deck must adopt the beatdown role it is not built for.
adjustments: Cantina Bouncer (owned x1) is strong here but always costs +2 with Chewbacca and a Cunning base because its second Cunning icon is uncovered. Use it only if deliberately accepting that penalty.
Repair x2 (owned) over Moment of Peace against burn-heavy fields.
acquisitions: Millennium Falcon — Piece of Junk (SOR) for a real space threat.

decks.name: Rogue Cell Initiative
decks.identity: This deck wins by making every enemy attack slightly unprofitable — Jyn's -1/-0 on defenders — while a wide Rebel board and exhaust effects win the initiative war one action at a time.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest with 11 space units; Outmaneuver can freeze the space arena for an alpha strike when the race is on.

guide.winCondition: Attrition-by-inches: units survive combats they shouldn't (Jyn), Bamboozle and Outmaneuver blank key attacks, and Dodonna-buffed Rebels grind the base down.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: Rebel bodies on curve; Battlefield Marine and Lothal Insurgent (hand disruption with any second card) are the best openers. Jyn's action makes an early trade favorable almost every round.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-5: Echo Base Defender walls; Rogue Operative's Saboteur+Raid 2 hits bases for 4 through Sentinels. Deploy Jyn at 6 — her passive turns every defensive block worse for them.
guide.lateGame: Round 5+: Outmaneuver exhausts an entire arena — set up two-round lethal. Wedge flashes vehicle reinforcements; Bright Hope re-buys a When Played unit and draws.
mulligan: Keep: two early Rebels or one plus interaction.
Keep: Lothal Insurgent + any 1-cost card (the disruption trigger needs a second play).
Send back: trick-heavy hands without a board.

combos.note: Defenders at -1/-0 mean the 1/2 trades with 2-power units; her death still banks an Experience token.
Exhaust their ground arena, then swing a +1/+1 Rebel board into nothing.
Strip the upgrades, peek the hand, discard their answer — tempo AND information.

cards: Mon Mothma — Voice of the Rebellion
combos.note: She digs 5 deep for the Rebel card that finishes; Assault doubles the payoff.

resourcePriorities: Resource Wedge/Snowspeeder before round 3
Keep one trick maximum in opening hands
Regional Sympathizers first out in fast matchups
weaknesses: FFG's retrospective rates Jyn low-impact among SOR leaders — tagged 'Experimental' honestly: the deck is fun and coherent, not top-tier.
Passive effects (Jyn, Dodonna) don't affect the board the round they arrive.
Split between tempo tricks and board plan; drawing the wrong half loses to focused decks.
adjustments: Ezra Bridger (owned x1) adds a Spectre value body over 1 Consortium StarViper.
Waylay 2nd/3rd copies (owned x6 total) if the meta is midrange ships.
acquisitions: K-2SO (SOR) — exactly this deck's kind of stubborn value.
Cassian Andor leader (SOR) would let this shell keep its identity with a stronger leader.

decks.name: Droid Kill Protocol
decks.identity: This deck wins by never having fewer units than the opponent — IG-88's whole kit pays you for width — and turning that census advantage into Raid-fueled swarm attacks.
decks.spacePolicy: Contest with 14 cheap space units; TIE/ln and Patrolling V-Wing exist to keep the unit count up in both arenas.

guide.winCondition: Board census: IG-88's action gives attackers +1/+0 whenever you have more units, and his deployed side grants everyone Raid 1. Forty-nine units make sure the count is always yours.
guide.earlyGame: Rounds 1-2: two units per round when possible (TIE/ln plus a 1-drop). Attack bases relentlessly; trades that cost you a unit but not them are usually wrong.
guide.midGame: Rounds 3-5: Veers and Motti make the swarm sting; Snowtrooper Lieutenant triggers extra attacks. Deploy IG-88 at 5 — every other unit gains Raid 1 and the math jumps.
guide.lateGame: Round 5+: Wampa and Imperial Interceptor are the whole top end — this deck wins round 6 or plays from behind. Count exact lethal early and spend everything toward it.
mulligan: Keep: any hand with three units costing 0-2.
Send back: anything with two cards costing 4+.
Never keep a hand that can't play a unit on round 1.

combos.note: Raid 2 plus leader-granted Raid 1 plus the census +1: the 0-power joke attacks for 4.
The Lieutenant's trigger sends the 3/1 in at 5 power on round 2.
Motti dies, the Wampa readies — Overwhelm twice in one round.
A cantrip that keeps the unit count up is exactly what the census plan wants.

resourcePriorities: Resource TIE Advanced and the 4-drops early
Never resource 1-cost units — they ARE the strategy
Open Fire is the only answer; keep it for Sentinels
weaknesses: FFG explicitly identified IG-88 as a weak SOR leader — this is the portfolio's 'Challenge' deck: play it for fun, not for the win rate.
One event total: zero answers to anything.
Any efficient multi-kill (Death Trooper, Barrage) breaks the census math.
adjustments: Open Fire can become Keep Fighting (owned x3) to re-swing after blocks.
Underworld Thug slots are flexible — Volunteer Soldier (owned x2) discounts itself with Troopers around.
acquisitions: For this deck, more copies of what it does: a 3rd TIE Advanced is owned (x4) — the real acquisitions budget belongs to the other 14 decks.

decks.name: Forge of the Rising Sun
decks.identity: Build one difficult-to-answer attacker, recycle upgrades through The Armorer, and keep enough bodies in both arenas to avoid all-in exposure.
decks.spacePolicy: Eight space units plus flexible upgrades keep enemy space pressure honest while the ground unit carries the forge.

guide.winCondition: Stack efficient upgrades on a protected unit, turn each attack into extra value, then close with a large Overwhelm or Sentinel threat.
guide.earlyGame: Develop a cheap unit before committing an upgrade; use The Armorer only when replacing the resource will not break the next turn.
guide.midGame: Move the fight toward your upgraded attacker and force unfavorable trades with combat tricks.
guide.lateGame: Finish through Trask Walker, Dinosaur Turtle, or a tall upgraded unit.
openingPriorities: Keep Bokken Saber or Durasteel Plating beside a cost-2 unit.
Prefer a hand with both ground and space access.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: Play the upgrade from resources, replace it, and keep developing without losing a resource.
A resilient Mandalorian body makes the conditional upgrade easier to preserve.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Hard removal can punish a single tall unit.
Too many upgrades without bodies creates non-functional hands.
adjustments: Against removal, spread upgrades across two units.
Against aggro, resource the slowest upgrade and contest immediately.

decks.name: Last Apprentice Standing
decks.identity: Baylan turns a lone arena threat into an oversized attacker; removal and Force units make that controlled isolation practical.
decks.spacePolicy: A compact space package prevents free damage while most pressure stays in ground where Baylan's Sentinel matters most.

guide.winCondition: Trade down to one premium unit in an arena, apply Baylan's +2/+2, and force the opponent through a Sentinel wall after deployment.
guide.earlyGame: Contest one arena cleanly instead of flooding both sides with fragile bodies.
guide.midGame: Use removal to preserve the lone-unit condition and attack for favorable trades.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Baylan behind a cleared arena and chain large Force and Imperial threats.
openingPriorities: Keep a cost-2 unit and one interaction spell.
Imperial Loyalist plus a cost-3 play is an ideal curve.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: A lone Elite becomes an awkwardly large target before Baylan deploys.
Layer targeted removal to restore Baylan's isolated-unit bonus.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Token swarms can make the isolated-unit plan inefficient.
Space-heavy decks can stretch the removal package.
adjustments: Against swarms, trade early rather than saving removal.
Against space, hold a space unit instead of resourcing it.

decks.name: Twin Suns Resurgence
decks.identity: Luke converts every attack into durability, letting efficient Heroism units win repeated combats before capital ships finish the game.
decks.spacePolicy: A balanced Rebel space suite makes Luke's healing relevant in either arena and supports a Home One finish.

guide.winCondition: Take favorable attacks that survive because of Luke's healing, accumulate board advantage, then close with Home One or Heroic Purrgil.
guide.earlyGame: Curve a Rebel unit into a second threat and heal whichever attack preserves the most future damage.
guide.midGame: Use Perseverance and resilient units to keep a wider board than the opponent.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Luke for an immediate heal, then overwhelm exhausted defenses with the surviving board.
openingPriorities: Keep Alliance X-Wing, Yoda, or Battlefield Marine.
Perseverance is a keep only with two early units.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: Repeated healing makes a key unit survive multiple combats.
The capital ship converts an established Rebel space plan into a final push.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Direct defeat effects bypass healing.
Very fast aggro can pressure the base before Luke deploys.
adjustments: Against hard removal, diversify threats.
Against aggro, prioritize board trades over base damage.

decks.name: Iron Discipline Doctrine
decks.identity: Sloane makes every body defensive and threatening: Sentinel stabilizes the base while Overwhelm converts trades into pressure.
decks.spacePolicy: The Imperial package is deliberately split between arenas so Sloane's global keywords constrain every attack lane.

guide.winCondition: Stabilize behind Sentinel bodies, grind value through combat, then deploy Sloane to make the whole board close at once.
guide.earlyGame: Play an Imperial body in the arena the opponent is developing and avoid unnecessary base races.
guide.midGame: Sequence removal before combat so Overwhelm damage reaches the base.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Sloane with multiple survivors and turn defense into a two-arena counterattack.
openingPriorities: Keep Vanguard Infantry, TIE/ln Fighter, or a cost-2 Sentinel candidate.
Keep Takedown against a known midrange opponent.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: Cheap bodies become both shields and Overwhelm attackers.
Removal buys the time needed to reach Sloane's deployed board.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Non-combat removal ignores Sentinel.
A sparse board reduces the value of Sloane's global text.
adjustments: Against control, deploy threats one at a time.
Against aggro, prioritize the arena with the largest immediate damage.

decks.name: Fulcrum Strike Web
decks.identity: Ahsoka lets the smaller unit punch above its cost, turning a broad Heroism board into efficient trades and sudden base damage.
decks.spacePolicy: Rebel space units carry Support bonuses safely while Mandalorians control the ground.

guide.winCondition: Maintain two differently sized units, use Ahsoka's buff and Support to win combat, then pivot the surviving board into base pressure.
guide.earlyGame: Develop two bodies before spending cards on tricks; a small unit is an asset because it activates Ahsoka.
guide.midGame: Attack in the order that preserves the power gap and maximizes Support.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Ahsoka to stack Support with her leader ability and force a decisive combat turn.
openingPriorities: Keep Battlefield Marine or Clan Wren Rescuer.
Prefer a hand with a cheap unit and a durable follow-up.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: The buffed smaller attacker becomes a legitimate removal tool.
Build a board first, then convert width into favorable combat.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Sweep effects punish overextension.
Equal-power boards can temporarily blank the leader ability.
adjustments: Against control, keep one unit in reserve.
Against aggro, use the buff for trades instead of base racing.

decks.name: Dead Man's Sightline
decks.identity: Cad's repeatable one-damage ping finishes wounded units and changes every combat calculation; Vigilance supplies the durable control shell.
decks.spacePolicy: TIE and capital-ship bodies ensure the control plan cannot be bypassed through space.

guide.winCondition: Chip opposing units into Cad range, trade efficiently, and let Overwhelm plus a stabilized board end the game.
guide.earlyGame: Play a body, then use Cad's ping only when it completes a defeat or creates a winning attack.
guide.midGame: Combine combat damage, Takedown, and the leader ping to gain card-neutral removals.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Cad, clear a defender with his attack trigger, and push Overwhelm damage through.
openingPriorities: Keep a cost-2 unit plus Takedown.
Against swarm decks, keep Force Choke.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: The ping brings larger units into Takedown range.
A sturdy body plus cheap removal helps Cad control combat.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Shield-heavy units can absorb pings.
Incorrect ping timing wastes the leader's main source of value.
adjustments: Against shields, attack them off before using Cad.
Against control, hold removal for deployed leaders.

decks.name: Corsair Fuse Lit
decks.identity: Vane treats upgrades as delayed direct damage, forcing the opponent to respect both enhanced units and a final burn sequence.
decks.spacePolicy: A broad pirate and Imperial space package creates safe carriers for disposable upgrades.

guide.winCondition: Attack with upgraded units, then cash in spent or endangered upgrades through Vane to finish the enemy base.
guide.earlyGame: Establish a cheap carrier before attaching an upgrade; avoid sacrificing an upgrade that can still win a combat.
guide.midGame: Pressure in both arenas and count the base damage available from Vane plus events.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Vane, sacrifice an upgrade during the decisive attack, and finish with burn or a capital ship.
openingPriorities: Keep Bokken Saber with a cost-2 unit.
Flarestar Attack Shuttle is a priority keep when the early curve is already present.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: Use the Saber in combat, then turn it into two base damage when it is no longer safe.
The high-end pair rewards the Command splash with a strong finish.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Sacrificing upgrades too early lowers total pressure.
The deck has fewer defensive tools than the control shells.
adjustments: Against aggro, use upgrades to win trades rather than burn.
Against control, spread carriers across arenas.

decks.name: Lothal Fast Talk
decks.identity: Ezra rewards clean three-damage base attacks by adding Advantage elsewhere, creating a tempo snowball across both arenas.
decks.spacePolicy: Cheap ships are central, not incidental: they provide the unblocked three-damage hit that turns on Ezra.

guide.winCondition: Land repeated three-plus base attacks, place Advantage where it wins the next action, and keep initiative pressure until a finisher lands.
guide.earlyGame: Develop in the less-contested arena and plan the attack order around Ezra's trigger.
guide.midGame: Use Cunning tricks to open a base lane, then move Advantage to the unit that attacks next.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Ezra for Saboteur and force through the last damage behind tempo events.
openingPriorities: Keep a cost-2 attacker with three power or a cheap space unit.
Rash Action is best kept only with a clear early curve.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: The power boost reliably crosses the three-damage threshold and creates Advantage.
A high-impact attacker plus initiative-focused tempo sustains pressure after the opening hand.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Sentinel can shut off the base-damage trigger.
Small attackers lose value if initiative is surrendered.
adjustments: Against Sentinel, save bounce and combat tricks for the blocking turn.
Against control, avoid deploying every threat at once.

decks.name: Beskar Initiative
decks.identity: Mandalorian bodies and Support attacks preserve initiative, turning the leader's paid draw into a steady source of cards.
decks.spacePolicy: Fang fighters and the Mandalorian Flagship ensure the tribal plan applies pressure above the ground fight.

guide.winCondition: Take initiative on carefully chosen turns, pay for extra cards, then use Support to turn a wide Mandalorian board into decisive attacks.
guide.earlyGame: Curve out and take initiative when the extra draw is worth more than one additional low-impact action.
guide.midGame: Build a mixed-arena board and use Mandalorian synergies to keep units alive.
guide.lateGame: Deploy the leader for Support and convert stored card advantage into a final two-arena push.
openingPriorities: Keep a cost-2 Mandalorian and a cost-3 follow-up.
Keep Intimidation against shield or upgrade strategies.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: Support makes the Flagship attack above rate while both cards reinforce the tribal plan.
Wide Mandalorian boards turn the event into a large combat swing.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Taking initiative too early can leave damage on the table.
Board clears punish the wide plan.
adjustments: Against control, stagger the high-value Mandalorians.
Against aggro, spend every action needed to stabilize before taking initiative.

decks.name: Imperial Advantage Engine
decks.identity: Palpatine turns a wide board into repeatable Advantage, rewarding cheap units, careful exhaustion, and precise attack ordering.
decks.spacePolicy: Cheap Imperial ships expand the board without congesting ground and provide safe Advantage recipients.

guide.winCondition: Build multiple units, exhaust the least useful one to grant Advantage to the key attacker, and snowball the extra combat stats.
guide.earlyGame: Play bodies before utility events; Palpatine is weakest when only one unit survives.
guide.midGame: Choose the exhausted unit and Advantage recipient before the first attack to preserve the best sequence.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Palpatine, create a large Advantage stack, and let surviving Imperials finish both arenas.
openingPriorities: Keep two cheap units, ideally split across arenas.
Diplomatic Pageantry is a keep only when the hand already develops the board.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: A cheap body can be exhausted to empower a much larger attacker.
The board-building package increases the number of units counted by Palpatine.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Sweep effects remove the resource Palpatine needs: board width.
Exhausting the wrong unit can sacrifice an important attack.
adjustments: Against control, maintain three threats without emptying the hand.
Against aggro, use Advantage to win trades before racing.

decks.name: Nevarro Profit Loop
decks.identity: Greef makes every played or created unit arrive with Advantage, so a unit-dense list turns ordinary development into immediate combat quality.
decks.spacePolicy: A high ship count makes Greef's universal unit trigger matter in both arenas and protects against one-dimensional boards.

guide.winCondition: Play units every turn, spend their Advantage in favorable attacks, and use the resulting board lead to close with large Heroism threats.
guide.earlyGame: Curve units rather than holding them for perfect value; each one already receives Greef's bonus.
guide.midGame: Attack with the unit whose Advantage will prevent the most return damage.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Greef and continue producing enhanced units until Heroic Purrgil ends the game.
openingPriorities: A mixed-arena opening is better than a hand of redundant ground units.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: Fresh units immediately receive the combat boost they need to contest the board.
The top end supplies both a hard-to-ignore threat and flexible late-game tempo.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: Unit-light draws waste the leader ability.
Hard control can answer one enhanced unit at a time.
adjustments: Against control, sequence medium threats before the Purrgil.
Against aggro, spend Advantage on trades rather than base damage.

decks.name: Small Foundling, Big Trouble
decks.identity: Grogu's deployed combat modifier turns close fights in either direction; the deck survives until that subtle math becomes a control engine.
decks.spacePolicy: Droids, smugglers, and Heroic Purrgil create a real space endgame while the ground side protects the base.

guide.winCondition: Defend efficiently, use Vigilance tricks to extend the game, then deploy Grogu and make every defender trade above rate.
guide.earlyGame: Prioritize durable units and avoid base racing; the deck is built to reach the deployed leader.
guide.midGame: Use bounce and defensive events only when they preserve a unit or deny a major attack.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Grogu, force combats into modified defenders, and win with Purrgil or a surviving wide board.
openingPriorities: Keep R2-D2, Leia Organa, or a cost-2 space unit.
Keep Fateful Goodbye against a known tall-unit deck.
mulligan: Keep at least two playable units costing 2–3; ship hands that start at cost 4.
Keep a key synergy card only when the hand already has an early unit.

combos.note: Tempo removal buys the turn needed for Grogu's defensive combat modifier to take over.
The low-cost Heroism pair provides early board presence and value.

resourcePriorities: Resource expensive finishers and duplicate situational events early.
Protect one copy of the card that unlocks the leader's central synergy.
weaknesses: The leader side supplies no early action.
Direct base damage can bypass the defensive combat plan.
adjustments: Against burn, pressure earlier with space units.
Against midrange, reserve interaction for the largest attacker.

decks.name: According to Plan
decks.identity: An aggressive counterpart to the go-wide value engine: the same board-wide Advantage payoff, but backed by Aggression removal so you clear blockers and load Palpatine's tokens onto a lethal attacker before the opponent stabilises.
decks.spacePolicy: A light TIE wing (Imperial Interceptor, TIE Advanced, TIE/ln Fighters) contests space so the ground swarm is not your only clock.

guide.winCondition: Flood the board with cheap Imperials, remove blockers with burn events, then use Palpatine to dump an Advantage token per other unit onto one exhausted attacker for a lethal swing.
guide.earlyGame: Play a one- or two-drop every turn. Prioritise contesting the ground and taking cheap trades; you want three or more bodies down before Palpatine deploys.
guide.midGame: Trade removal (Open Fire, Force Choke) for the opponent's best blockers and keep attacking. Snowtrooper Lieutenant's free attack pushes extra Imperial damage.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Palpatine and, on a wide board, hand a stack of Advantage tokens to a single exhausted unit to close the game in one attack.
openingPriorities: Two one-cost units and a two-drop.
At least one removal event to answer an early blocker.
mulligan: Keep any hand with two units at cost 1-2.
Ship hands with no play before turn three or only high-cost cards.

combos.note: A full swarm lets Palpatine load several Advantage tokens onto one exhausted attacker, turning a 2-cost body into a finisher.
Saboteur plus the Trooper bonus punches a 3/1 through Sentinels and Shields for efficient removal or direct base damage.

resourcePriorities: Resource duplicate high-cost units and situational events first.
Keep two removal events in hand once the board is wide.
weaknesses: Board wipes undo the go-wide plan.
A slow start leaves Palpatine's payoff without enough bodies.
adjustments: Against control, hold a second wave back after a wipe.
Against aggro, use Open Fire and Force Choke defensively before racing.

decks.name: Quickdraw Contract
decks.identity: A fast, damage-first take on Cad Bane: instead of the Vigilance control shell, run the whole Aggression/Villainy beatdown package and let Bane's repeatable ping finish anything combat leaves standing.
decks.spacePolicy: Imperial Interceptor, TIE Advanced and TIE/ln Fighters keep a foothold in space; the main clock is on the ground.

guide.winCondition: Curve out efficient attackers, trade up with burn, and use Cad Bane's ping to finish wounded units so your board survives and keeps hitting the base.
guide.earlyGame: Deploy a unit every turn and attack into whatever you can kill; Bane's ping plus a 1-cost attack often removes a two-drop for free.
guide.midGame: Use Open Fire and Force Choke on key blockers, then push Overwhelm damage through with Wampa and the AT-ST.
guide.lateGame: Close with Overwhelm attackers while Bane snipes the last HP off blockers; Wolffe shuts off base healing for the final turns.
openingPriorities: A one-drop and a two-drop attacker.
A removal event for an early wall.
mulligan: Keep two early units.
Ship reactive-only hands or all four-plus drops.

combos.note: Soften a blocker with Open Fire, then Bane's ping finishes it without committing an attacker — every combat maths in your favour.
Wolffe stops the opponent healing their base while Overwhelm converts trades into a fast base clock.

resourcePriorities: Resource the six-cost AT-STs and spare copies of situational events.
Keep a ping target in mind before spending Bane's action.
weaknesses: Runs low on gas against dedicated control.
Efficient Sentinel walls slow the ground plan.
adjustments: Against go-wide, prioritise Open Fire on their payoff unit.
Against ramp, race and use Wolffe to deny base healing.

decks.name: Red Five Offensive
decks.identity: A proactive alternative to the healing-value Luke build: a wide Rebel/Aggression board that attacks every turn while Luke's leader heal erases the chip damage, so your units win the same combat again and again.
decks.spacePolicy: Alliance X-Wings and Restored ARC-170s hold space and chip the base; ground is the primary damage plan.

guide.winCondition: Establish a wide Rebel board, buff it with General Dodonna and Experience tokens, and attack relentlessly while Luke heals the survivors after each combat.
guide.earlyGame: Curve out with two-power bodies and take early trades; Luke's heal makes attacking into slightly larger units correct.
guide.midGame: Land General Dodonna to raise the whole team, then use Fleet Lieutenant's free attacks and Medal Ceremony to bank Experience.
guide.lateGame: Deploy Luke, keep your best attackers topped up with his heal, and use Vanquish to clear the one blocker in your way.
openingPriorities: Two units at cost 1-2 and a three-drop.
General Dodonna or Fleet Lieutenant to scale the board.
mulligan: Keep hands with an early curve.
Ship removal-heavy hands with no units before turn three.

combos.note: A 3/7 that attacks each turn while Luke heals the chip damage is almost impossible to remove in combat.
Dodonna's +1/+1 anthem plus a free Rebel attack turns a wide board into efficient, repeated pressure.

resourcePriorities: Resource duplicate finishers and spare Experience payoffs.
Hold Vanquish for the blocker that stops your best attacker.
weaknesses: Board wipes hit the wide plan hard.
Losing Dodonna deflates the whole team.
adjustments: Against control, commit in waves and keep Luke's Lightsaber for a resilient threat.
Against aggro, use early bodies and Luke's heal to stabilise before turning the corner.
acquisitions: A second copy of key Rebel legends
